subject: [PATCH 3/3] fuse: end fuse_req on io-uring cancel task work

reviews.author: Bernd Schubert
reviews.summary: Reviewer Bernd Schubert noted that the initial code used IO_URING_F_TASK_DEAD, which he had incorrectly assumed was related to PF_EXITING, and suggested that fuse_uring_cancel() and canceled fuse_uring_send_in_task() should go through fuse_uring_entry_teardown()
reviews.raw_body:
I see, the initial code was using IO_URING_F_TASK_DEAD and I had wrongly 
assumed that is related to PF_EXITING. 
Well, I think the fix is clear, although I personally do not like the
exit code dup (or better triple)
https://lore.kernel.org/r/20260515045541.1171335-4-joannelkoong@gmail.com

In my option fuse_uring_cancel() and canceled fuse_uring_send_in_task() 
should go through fuse_uring_entry_teardown().


Thanks,
Bernd

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that canceling a task work doesn't necessarily imply connection abort and agreed to free the ent immediately on cancel, but expressed neutrality about the approach.
sentiment_signals: open-ended phrase 'I don't feel strongly about this'
reviews.raw_body:
I don't feel strongly about this. On cancel, the ent becomes useless
since cancel consumes the cmd, so imo it makes sense to just free and
clean up the ent immediately instead of keeping it around until the
fuse connection gets eventually aborted, but I don't think it really
matters. Happy to go with what you prefer.

Thanks,
Joanne

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the patch needs to be minimal and agreed to send a v2 with only the necessary changes, specifically avoiding deduping in this version.
reviews.raw_body:
This fix (as well as [1]) needs to be backported to stable so I think
it'd be better to keep the fix as minimal as possible and do any
entry_teardown deduping as a follow-up cleanup after that. I'll send
out a v2 that fixes it minimally.

Thanks,
Joanne

[1] https://lore.kernel.org/fuse-devel/20260516021138.2759874-4-joannelkoong@gmail.com/

2026-06-08.patch_summary: This patch ensures that a fuse request is properly ended when an io-uring cancel task work occurs, even if it doesn't result in a subsequent fuse connection abort. This prevents the application's syscall thread from becoming stuck uninterruptibly in D-state for the lifetime of the connection. The change addresses a specific edge case where tw.cancel happens without a corresponding fuse abort, which is not the common scenario but still needs to be handled.
